title: 4 Customizing Wires Elements
body_text:
4	Customizing Wires Elements
4.1	Overview of Customizing Wires Elements
Certain aspects of the Wires application can be customized. This chapter describes the customizable elements and how to request changes.
4.2	Description of Wires Customizable Elements
You can customize the following Wires elements:
FINTRAC Details
FINTRAC details allow you to collect additional information about the sender and beneficiary. Completion of the fields isnГўВҖВҷt mandatory. See Figure 1 for an example of the sender FINTRAC Details section.
Figure 1: Sender FINTRAC Details Section
You can customize the FINTRAC details to:
Always Show
ГўВҖВ“ The FINTRAC fields always display.
Let Logic Decide
ГўВҖВ“ The FINTRAC fields display when Wires detects that an EFT Report is required.
Note:	FINTRAC fields differ if the sender is a business/entity.
Representative/Conductor Details
When a representative of a business or entity (e.g., an employee) sends a wire on behalf of the business or entity, you can collect information about the sender in the Representative/Conductor Details section. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Representative/Conductor Details Section
You can customize the Representative/Conductor Details section to be:
Enabled
ГўВҖВ“ The Representative/Conductor Details section displays when you select Business/Entity as the sender type.
Disabled
ГўВҖВ“ The Representative/Conductor Details section doesnГўВҖВҷt display.
Note:	The Representative/Conductor Details section is disabled by default.
Politically Exposed Person (PEP)/Head of International Organization (HIO) Determination Questions
The PEP/HIO determination questions are used to ascertain if the sender of a very large wire ($100,000 Canadian equivalent or greater) is a PEP or an HIO. The user records the senderГўВҖВҷs responses in Wires. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: PEP/HIO Questions
You can customise the PEP/HIO determination questions to be:
Enabled
ГўВҖВ“ The questions display in the Regulatory section for very large wires ($100,000 Canadian equivalent or greater). Completion of the questions are required.
Disabled
ГўВҖВ“ The questions donГўВҖВҷt display. If you select this option, you must record the PEP/HIO determination information separately.
Collect Funding Method
Collect funding method displays in the regulatory section and provides the following:
A drop-down menu to record how the sender is paying for the wire.
A question displays asking the user if information about the source of funds (person/entity) was obtained. If the user selects ГўВҖВңYesГўВҖВқ, they must enter how the requester initially acquired the funds used for the wire (e.g., employment, sale of large asset, gift). See Figure 4.
Figure 4: The Collect Funding Method Question
You can customize the collect funding method field to be:
Enabled
ГўВҖВ“ The collect funding method drop-down menu displays in the Regulatory section if required. Completion of the field is mandatory.
Disabled
ГўВҖВ“ The collect funding method drop-down menu doesnГўВҖВҷt display. If you select this option, you must record information about the payment method separately.
Customized Message Boxes
You can customize the message box text on the following pages in Wires:
Create Wire
ГўВҖВ“ Displays at the bottom of the Create Regular Wire page.
Confirmation
ГўВҖВ“ Displays at the bottom of the Sent Wire Transfer ГўВҖВ“ Confirmation page.
Receipt
ГўВҖВ“ Displays at the bottom of the Receipt page. The Receipt page displays after you create a wire.
Note: 	You canГўВҖВҷt customize the terms and conditions text on the printable wire confirmation and receipt.
Use the message boxes to add applicable messaging and/or to provide a link to supporting reference material (e.g., an internal waiver). See Figure 5, Figure 6, and Figure 7 for samples.
Note:	There are no character limits in the message boxes, however longer messages affect page display.
Figure 5: Create Regular Wire Message Box
Figure 6: Send Wire Transfer Page Confirmation Message Box
Figure 7: Receipt Page Message Box
FINTRAC Fields Message Box
You can customize the text in the message box that displays beside the FINTRAC details fields. See Figure 8. The FINTRAC details fields display according to how you customize the Wires application. See the FINTRAC Details category in this section.
Note:	There arenГўВҖВҷt character limits in the message box, however longer messages affect page display.
Figure 8: FINTRAC Fields Message Box
Regulatory Message Box
You can customize the text in the message box that displays when you select
Yes
on PEP/HIO questions. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Regulatory Fields Message Box
Wires Fee Option (Customer Fees)
Specify how your customer fees are applied in the Wires application.
Note:	Use the Fees option in Wires to update outgoing wire fees. See Chapter 11, Managing Outgoing Wire Fees.
Manual entry
ГўВҖВ“ Users enter a fee manually for each wire. You donГўВҖВҷt need to complete the fee table.
Pre-populated
ГўВҖВ“ Outgoing wire fees and add-on fees. Wires automatically populates the outgoing wire fee in the Fee field based on criteria that you define. Wires Users can manually overwrite a pre-populated fee.
Outgoing Wire Fees
Fees are based on the criteria that you define. Users can manually overwrite the pre-populated fee in Wires. You must define the following criteria for applying the fee.
ГўВҲВ’
Maximum Wire Amount
ГўВҖВ“ The maximum wire amount to apply a defined fee. You can define fees based on the maximum wire amount, in tiers, up to $50,000. If you define fees in tiers, you must also define fees for wires greater than $50,000. Enter ГўВҖВңNoneГўВҖВқ if the wire amount isnГўВҖВҷt used to determine the fee, or to define fees for wire amounts over $50,000.
ГўВҲВ’
Wire Currency
ГўВҖВ“ The currency of the wire. The options available are CAD, USD, and ANY. You can define separate fees for wires in Canadian and/or US dollars currencies. Use ГўВҖВңANYГўВҖВқ to apply an applicable fee to all wire currencies or, if youГўВҖВҷve defined a fee for Canadian and/or US dollar wires, to define a fee for all other currencies.
ГўВҲВ’
Destination Country
ГўВҖВ“ The destination country for the defined wire fee. The options available are CA, US, and ANY. You can define a separate fee for wires destined for Canada and/or the United States. Use ГўВҖВңANYГўВҖВқ to apply the applicable fee to all destination countries or, if youГўВҖВҷve defined a fee for wires destined for Canada and/or the US, to define a fee for all other currencies.
ГўВҲВ’
Fee
ГўВҖВ“ The customer fee to be pre-populated in the wire fee field based on the defined criteria. A user can manually overwrite the pre-populated fee in Wires.
ГўВҲВ’
Fee Currency
ГўВҖВ“ The currency in which to charge the fee. The options available are CAD and USD. You must have a US dollar Central 1 current account to apply a fee in US dollars.
In the sample below (Figure 10), fees for wires up to $10,000 are defined as follows:
ГўВҲВ’
Wires destined for Canada, in Canadian currency, have a defined fee of $12.00 charged in Canadian funds.
ГўВҲВ’
Wires destined for the United States in US currency, have a defined fee of $12.00 charged in US funds.
ГўВҲВ’
Wires destined for all other countries, in any other currency, have a defined fee of $13.00 charged in Canadian funds.
A similar structure was followed for wires between $10,000 and $50,000 and for wires above $50,000.
Figure 10: Sample Fee Table
Add-on Wire Fees
Fees for future-dated and priority wires. Applies to financial institutions that enable pre-populated fees in the Wires application. Wires automatically includes the appropriate add-on fee in the Fee field when users create a future-dated or high priority wire. Add-on fees are based on the criteria that you define. See Figure 11.
Figure 11. Add-on Fees
Important
: 	Central 1 charges your financial institution a processing fee per wire at month end. Processing fees donГўВҖВҷt include the fee that you charge your customer. To recover your processing costs, ensure the fee that you charge your customer (pre-populated or manually entered) is, at minimum, the value of the Central 1 processing fee.
4.3	Requesting Wires Customization Changes
To request changes to your Wires customization:
Complete the Wires Customization Request, Form 6493, and forward as directed. See Figure 12, Figure 13, and Figure 14.
Figure 12: Wires Customization Request, Form 6493, Page 1
Figure 13: Wires Customization Request, Form 6493, Page 2
Figure 14: Wires Customization Request, Form 6493, Page 3
Related Information
Wires Customization Request,
Form 6493
4.4	Wires Data Extract Service
The Wires Data Extract Service provides subscribing financial institutions with an XML data file and a report file for FINTRAC reporting purposes. Your financial institution is responsible for analysis of the data and for reporting the transactions to FINTRAC.
Note:	The Wires Data Extract Service is subject to fees. Financial institutions must have access to their own folder on Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange to use this service.
Subscribing financial institutions download the XML file and report file from Central 1ГўВҖВҷs File and Report Exchange after 8:00 pm PT / 11:00 pm ET on the business day after the transaction date. You can import the data file to an AML system or send the file to an AML service provider. The information in the file is used to determine which incoming and outgoing wires are reported to FINTRAC.
N
ote: 	If Central 1 canГўВҖВҷt determine the cross-border status of a wire, the cross-border field in the Wires Data Extract displays as ГўВҖВңVerify ManuallyГўВҖВқ and the receiving financial institution or your AML vendor will need to manually determine the country of the sending financial institution. Your financial institution is responsible for reviewing and reporting wires to FINTRAC.
To request, update, or cancel the Wires Data Extract Service:
Complete the Wires Data Extract Service Request, Form 2592. See Figure 15. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
Figure 15: Wires Data Extract Service Request, Form 2592, Page 1
Forward the form as indicated on page 2. If applicable, the central credit union will authorize the form and forward it to Central 1.
Note:	The Wires Data Extract Service is referred to as ГўВҖВңWTS Data Extract Services (Optional)ГўВҖВқ in the Wires agreement.
Related Information
Wires Data Extract Service Request,
Form 2592
Wires Data Extract Service File Specifications,
Form 9327
END
